rationale.R1.2: A general section review centrally includes checking whether the argument moves coherently from motivation to benchmark results to conclusion.
rationale.R1.3: The section makes quantitative performance and bias-reduction claims, so review responsibility includes checking whether reported numbers support those claims.
rationale.R1.6: Because the delegation asks to review a written section, sentence-level clarity, register, and concision are central expected responsibilities.
rationale.RX.1: The reviewer should disclose uncertainty where the artifact lacks methodological or statistical detail needed to verify claims.
rationale.RX.2: The reviewer should flag language such as broad effectiveness or survey-agnostic conclusions if it exceeds the evidence presented.
rationale.RX.3: The review must stay within the provided section rather than executing revisions or evaluating unavailable parts of the paper.
rationale.RX.4: The reviewer should avoid recommendations that could encourage misleading scientific claims or unsupported deployment conclusions.
rationale.RX.5: Any review judgments should be traceable to specific wording, claims, or reported metrics in the section.
